title: cuscuta tinctoria var. aurea
intro: Cuscuta tinctoria var. aurea, also known as golden dodder, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Dodder family (Cuscutaceae) and is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exerting antioxidant effects, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Cuscuta tinctoria var. aurea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
